learning.summary: A clean install removes the existing operating system and data from the target partition before installing a new operating system. It is commonly used to resolve major software problems or start with a fresh system.
learning.explanation:
A clean install erases the contents of the selected partition and installs a new copy of the operating system. Because existing files, applications, and settings are removed, it is considered a destructive installation method.

Before performing a clean install, important data should be backed up so it can be restored afterward. Clean installs are often used when replacing an operating system, removing persistent software issues, or preparing a computer for a new user.

facts.text: A clean install deletes the existing operating system and data on the target partition.
Important data should be backed up before performing a clean install.
A clean install provides a fresh operating system installation.

examples.text: A technician backs up user documents, formats the Windows partition, and installs a fresh copy of Windows.

examTips: Know that a clean install is destructive and requires backups.
commonMistakes: Performing a clean install without backing up user data.
scenarios: Recommend a clean install for a malware-infected system that cannot be reliably repaired.
pbqIdeas: Choose the appropriate installation type and backup steps.

reviewNotes: Backup guidance was enriched beyond the explicit transcript evidence per Discovery Review GAP-003.
